name: BTC Squeeze Hold Release v1
edge_mechanism: volatility squeeze expansion confirmed by persistence instead of first breakout bar
thesis: BTC 1h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing squeeze release entered only after post-break persistence. In a mixed intraday regime, the first breakout bar is often a trap; the edge should come from entering only when price proves it can hold above fast value after the release.
invalidation_condition: Abort if squeeze releases are too sparse, if post-break holds fail to extend, or if the persistence filter still leaves the strategy as a false-break collector.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Compression resolves into expansion, price holds above fast value after the release, and then continues far enough for a modest second leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Choppy intraday markets still produce periodic compression-release episodes. Waiting for persistence should reduce false first-bar breakouts and preserve cleaner second-leg continuations.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is the best clean lane in the basket for testing a persistence-gated squeeze release.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Compression may release often enough, but if most holds snap back quickly the persistence filter will still not rescue expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are valid but travel is short, reduce target ambition before changing the squeeze definition.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Selective entries with a decent first-target hit rate and occasional modest runner contribution.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 1h and one cross-lane show tradable density and positive follow-through after the post-break hold.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or persistence holds that repeatedly fail to extend.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; squeeze and hold are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by moderate squeeze thresholds rather than extreme rarity

test_lanes.why_included: Best clean lane for compression release persistence.
test_lanes.continue_if: Release-hold setups show enough density and positive follow-through after costs.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Setups are too sparse or holds fail to extend.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: A modest number of clean persistence entries after release.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism transfers to the primary basket asset.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Similar but slightly noisier post-break persistence.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the persistence filter survives on a wilder lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS preserves the mechanism without degenerating into chaos.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger persistence events than BTC.

long: Exit remaining 20% on close back through EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on close back through EMA_55 or 4 bars
